description:
Advances: specled.spec_review

lib/specled_ex/review.ex + review/html.ex: Coverage tab per requirement — 'Closure: N MFAs — K executed (X%). Self-verified: yes/no. Tagged tests: T1 (executed), T2 (linked).'; subject-card rollup badge; 'Reached by tests' row only when mode == :per_test; honest banners for :invalid_artifact / :legacy_artifact (incl. generated_at staleness display). lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex: read envelope; print mode + closure-coverage %; label per-test detectors unavailable in aggregate mode.

## Orchestrator addenda (admission, epic @ 2bc0fc8)

Landed on your base — your job is to wire these into rendering (they currently have ZERO production callers, by design): CoverageTriangulation.envelope_findings/3, CoverageTriangulation.aggregate_requirement_reach/2, Review.CoverageClosure.build_v2/2 (T6), over v2 envelopes from Store.read_v2/read_status (T1/T2/T3). Maintainer decision 1 (binding): consumers are mix spec.triangle + mix spec.review ONLY — nothing wires into mix spec.check.

Two flags from T6's verification you MUST address:
1. CoverageClosure.build_v2 tags a degraded :per_test envelope :ok_per_test (no async-contamination special-case). Rendering must show degraded per-test data as DEGRADED, honestly — prefer render-side handling from envelope.degraded; if that is impossible without misrepresenting state, a minimal status special-case in coverage_closure.ex is conditionally authorized (flag it in your completion record).
2. per_test MFA coverage is currently a file-level proxy pending specled_-155.5 (in flight). Do not over-claim precision in the UI: label per-test-derived MFA coverage accordingly until T5's real data lands (a short qualifier in the tab is fine).

Sibling in flight: specled_-155.5 owns formatter.ex/coverage.ex/snapshot.ex/spec.cover.test.ex — do not touch those.

Spec co-change is authorized and required when the gate demands it; .spec/realization_hashes.json is tool-managed. A green gate outranks Out of Scope.

LESSONS:
- append new code below existing api_boundary-bound functions (specled_-wp7, from 155.1)
- mix compile --force before spec.check seeds hashes for brand-new bound functions (from 155.6)

## Allowed touches

- lib/specled_ex/review.ex
- lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex
- lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex (ONLY the conditional degraded-status special-case per addendum flag 1, if render-side is impossible)
- priv/spec_review_assets/** (only if the tab needs styling hooks)
- test/specled_ex/review_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review/html_layout_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs (or the repo's actual spec.triangle test file), test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs (only if flag-1 authorization used)
- .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md (only sections your diff invalidates + stub flips)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/** (all of it), lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.ingest.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex (CALL, never edit)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (decision 1)
- docs/**

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No new external deps (prism/assets are vendored — no CDN references; artifact-style CSP thinking applies: everything self-contained)
- No data-layer changes beyond the single conditionally-authorized special-case
- Nothing wired into mix spec.check

## Verification

- Coverage tab (aggregate mode): renders 'Closure: N MFAs — K executed (X%)', self-verified yes/no, tagged test names with evidence strengths — asserted against a constructed envelope + closure fixture in html tests
- Subject-card rollup badge present
- 'Reached by tests' row rendered ONLY when mode == :per_test
- :legacy_artifact / :invalid_artifact / :no_coverage_artifact render as distinct honest banners (never fake 0% or empty-but-ok); legacy banner names mix spec.cover.test
- Degraded :per_test envelope renders as degraded (flag 1)
- generated_at staleness displayed in the tab
- mix spec.triangle prints mode + closure-coverage %; per-test detectors labeled detector_unavailable in aggregate mode
- Existing review/html/layout/task tests green
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors, targeted tests, full mix spec.check all green

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.7/codex-impl-1/log

Commit: 28fe53305845aa6b40b3530480e648e32299911a
Files touched: .spec/realization_hashes.json, .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md, lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex, lib/specled_ex/review.ex, lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex, lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review/html_layout_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review_test.exs
Tests added:
  - SpecLedEx.Review.HtmlTest "render_coverage_tab — v2 envelope bind-closure view" (aggregate line format, self-verified yes/no, tagged-tests w/ strength, mode-gated "Reached by tests" row, per_test file-level-proxy qualifier, empty-closure form, distinct banners for no_coverage_artifact/no_tracer_manifest/legacy_artifact/invalid_artifact/async_contaminated, generated_at + staleness, backward-compat no-closure_reach-key)
  - SpecLedEx.Review.HtmlTest "render_subject_coverage_badge — subject-card rollup badge" (self-verified/total + mode label; muted unavailable chip per degraded status)
  - SpecLedEx.ReviewTest "Coverage tab bind-closure integration" — new test "wires CoverageClosure.build_v2/2 into the Coverage pivot (v2 line format + generated_at)"; existing 3 tests updated from stale v1 :coverage_records opt to v2 :envelope opt
  - SpecLedEx.Review.CoverageClosureTest "build_v2/2 — status gating" — new tests for :async_contaminated (degraded :per_test never reports :ok_per_test) and non-degraded :per_test still reporting :ok_per_test
  - Mix.Tasks.SpecTriangleTest "v2 envelope: mode + closure-coverage % + detector_unavailable labeling" — aggregate mode (mode:+closure_coverage+aggregate_artifact_only label), missing artifact (preserves old wording), legacy_artifact, invalid_artifact, async-contaminated per_test, non-degraded per_test (v1 diagnostic unchanged)
Verification stubs flipped: none (no execute:false stubs existed for the touched requirements)
coverage_closure.ex special-case used: yes — flag 1. build_v2/2 exposed no channel (status atom or otherwise) for a degraded :per_test envelope; render-side detection was impossible without a data change, so build_v2/2 now short-circuits `{:ok, %{mode: :per_test, degraded: true}}` to a new `:async_contaminated` v2_status (empty by_requirement, same shape as the other degraded statuses) before the normal :ok_per_test path.
Verification tier: change-scoped — ran every test file the diff touches (review_test.exs, review/html_test.exs, review/html_layout_test.exs, review/coverage_closure_test.exs, mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs, mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs) plus the sibling-covered targets named in spec_review.spec.md's coverage_tab_bind_closure verification (coverage_triangulation_test.exs, review/findings_delta_test.exs, review/file_diff_test.exs) — 248 tests, 0 failures. Full mix spec.check (status=pass, 2 pre-existing ratified info findings unrelated to this diff) and mix compile --force --warnings-as-errors both green. Full-system proof (whole suite) deferred to the merge gate / PR CI per the skill's iteration-vs-completion tiering.
Final-tier runs: 1 (green on first run; no retries needed)

Summary: Wired CoverageTriangulation.envelope_findings/3, aggregate_requirement_reach/2, and CoverageClosure.build_v2/2 into their first production callers — mix spec.review's Coverage tab (new v2 line format, self-verified, tagged-test evidence, mode-gated reached-by-tests row, subject rollup badge, generated_at staleness, distinct degraded banners) and mix spec.triangle (mode + closure-coverage % + detector_unavailable labeling). Addressed both orchestrator-flagged addenda: a new :async_contaminated status (flag 1) so a corrupted --per-test capture never renders as trustworthy, and an explicit file-level-proxy qualifier on per_test closure lines (flag 2) pending specled_-155.5. Updated coverage_tab_bind_closure's spec wording to match and reseeded the one legitimate api_boundary hash change in realization_hashes.json.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: verify-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.7/codex-verify-1/log

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 12
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
- spec.check wiring check (decision 1): clean — spec.check.ex UNTOUCHED and has zero references to build_v2/2, aggregate_requirement_reach/2, or envelope_findings/3; consumers are mix spec.review + mix spec.triangle only
- coverage_closure.ex conditional authorization: justified — base build_v2/2 returned only {status, by_requirement} and collapsed a degraded :per_test envelope to :ok_per_test with no channel (status or otherwise) exposing degraded; the renderer receives only per-subject {status, by_requirement}, so honest degraded rendering required a data-layer status. Change is minimal (+12 lines: a distinct :async_contaminated status, empty by_requirement, same shape as sibling degraded statuses), documented, and flagged in the completion record. Advisory: review.ex separately re-reads the envelope for generated_at, so render-side detection was not strictly *impossible*; but placing the mode/degraded decision in the data layer is the more honest choice and the authorization covers it.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: all verification blocks on touched requirements are execute:true (no execute:false stubs existed); exercised via the targeted test commands, all green
- Requirements coverage:
  - specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure (must): covered by html_test.exs (aggregate line "Closure: N MFAs — K executed (X.X%)" :82; self-verified yes/no :83,:123; tagged tests w/ strength :124; Reached-by-tests row per_test-only + refute in aggregate :90,:127; file-level-proxy qualifier :181 + refute in aggregate :93; generated_at + staleness :243-:265) and coverage_triangulation_test.exs. Would fail if the aggregate line dropped the executed/pct segment, the reached-by-tests row leaked into aggregate mode, the proxy qualifier were absent on per_test, or generated_at staleness were not flagged. Seams: reach maps / envelope injected via opts (bypasses real Store.read_v2 load path — cross-ticket seam with T1/T2/T5).
  - specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_v2_envelope_data_layer (must): covered by coverage_closure_test.exs status-gating tests (:async_contaminated asserted, and explicitly != :ok_per_test; non-degraded per_test still :ok_per_test) + html render banner tests (:legacy_artifact :215, :invalid_artifact :226, :async_contaminated :238 — each refutes cov-closure rows) + subject rollup badge (:296, unavailable chip :312). Would fail if a degraded :per_test rendered as trustworthy per-test data, or the distinct statuses collapsed into one banner / fake 0% / empty-but-ok. Seams: envelope injected via opt.
  - specled.triangulation.spec_triangle_task (should): covered by spec_triangle_test.exs (mode + closure_coverage % + detector_unavailable labeling incl. aggregate_artifact_only, missing/legacy/invalid/async-contaminated notes) and confirmed by live runs (absent-artifact prints distinct no_coverage_artifact note; aggregate mode prints closure_coverage). Would fail if mode were not printed, aggregate closure_coverage % omitted, or per-test detectors silently dropped.
- Reworded-requirement judgment: equivalent-or-stronger. old classifies :shall, new classifies :shall, downgrade?=false. The positive phrasing ("stays absent", "never collapsing", "renders exclusively when") deliberately avoids "shall not", which the classifier (negative-precedence, cross-polarity positive->negative = conservative downgrade) would flip to :shall_not and false-positive against a :shall base — story verified against modal_class.ex. New statement is strictly additive (self-verified, tagged-tests-with-strength, rollup badge, generated_at staleness, four distinct banners, file-level-proxy qualifier) and preserves the anti-misrepresentation/degraded-piggyback guarantee; aggregate mode omitting per-test rows is honest, not a weakening.
- Production-wiring trace: build_v2/2 -> review.ex:63 (mix spec.review); aggregate_requirement_reach/2 -> spec.triangle.ex:374 + coverage_closure.ex:485 (build_v2 aggregate path, reached via spec.review); envelope_findings/3 -> spec.triangle.ex:310,333,386. All three have real callers; none zero-caller.
- Hash reseed check: mix compile --force + mix spec.check run twice — run2 and run3 status=pass errors=0 warnings=0 exit 0, working tree clean. build_v2/2 body genuinely changed vs base (added {:ok, %{mode: :per_test, degraded: true}} clause), so the single api_boundary hash update (6f1c...->d495...) is a legitimate content reseed, not a wp7 line-leak. NOTE: spec.check run1 hit an environmental flake — the command-runner wrote a temp target.sh that failed with "exs: command not found" (exit 127) on the UNTOUCHED specled.evidence_store subject; that exact command passes manually (26 tests, 0 failures) and the same "specled_cmd_XXXX.sh: No such file or directory" temp-script race appears during mix test --stale (which still passes 1121/0). Not caused by this diff.
- Governance checks: clean. 2 branch-leg findings are info-level (gate status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0); no new errors/warnings.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none — no new deps (mix.exs/mix.lock untouched), generated HTML self-contained (no http/https/cdn/script/link refs added; CSS inlined in html.ex), nothing wired into mix spec.check.
- Targeted verification: pass. Commands: mix test (9 files, 248/0); mix test --stale (1121/0); mix spec.triangle <subject> --artifact-path <absent> (distinct no_coverage_artifact banner + detector_unavailable note); mix spec.triangle <subject> (default); mix help spec.triangle (updated v2 docs present).
- Corpus-scoped claims: 3 validated (coverage_tab_bind_closure rewording, coverage_tab_v2_envelope_data_layer status set, spec_triangle_task v2 diagnostic) — modal classification validated by execution, semantics by whole-file old-vs-new comparison; no falsehoods.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- The per_test MFA-coverage path is a file-level proxy (flag 2) with an honest UI qualifier; real MFA-level data lands with specled_-155.5. Tests inject the envelope via the :envelope opt, so the Store.read_v2 -> envelope.degraded producer contract (T1/T2) is a cross-ticket integration seam not exercised here.
- spec.check exhibits an intermittent command-runner temp-script race (unrelated to this ticket). Worth tracking separately if it recurs on the merge gate.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: 0a6853df-a850-4f0b-9cf5-09b2923d30b8 (orchestrator)
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mzubieta-dev-mz-specled-ex/0a6853df-a850-4f0b-9cf5-09b2923d30b8.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full requirement coverage, zero scope
violations (coverage_closure.ex ruled justified under the flag-1 conditional
authorization), passing targeted verification including live-path task runs,
complete seam disclosure, and no seam substituting production wiring. The
reworded requirement was proven equivalent-or-stronger by executing the modal
classifier. No auditor spawned per policy.

title: T7: spec.review + spec.triangle rendering of spec coverage
